title: Ensure That App Engine Applications Enforce HTTPS  Connections

input_row.title: Ensure That App Engine Applications Enforce HTTPS  Connections
input_row.description: In order to maintain the highest level of security all connections to an application should be secure by default.
input_row.rationale: Insecure HTTP connections maybe subject to eavesdropping which can expose sensitive data.
input_row.audit: Verify that the app.yaml file controlling the application contains a line which enforces secure connections. For example handlers: - url: /.* secure: always redirect_http_response_code: 301 script: auto https://cloud.google.com/appengine/docs/standard/python3/config/appref
input_row.remediation: Add a line to the app.yaml file controlling the application which enforces secure connections. For example handlers: - url: /.* **secure: always** redirect_http_response_code: 301 script: auto [https://cloud.google.com/appengine/docs/standard/python3/config/appref]
input_row.impact: All connections to appengine will automatically be redirected to the HTTPS endpoint ensuring that all connections are secured by TLS.
input_row.default_value: By default both HTTP and HTTP are supported

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. Open the Google Cloud Console and navigate to "App Engine" in the left-side menu.
2. Select the application you want to check from the dashboard.
3. Navigate to the "Settings" section in the App Engine area.
4. Identify and locate the `app.yaml` file for your application. This file is typically found in your application's source code repository.
5. Open the `app.yaml` file and look for the `handlers` section.
6. Verify that there is a line present that enforces secure connections. The line should look like this:
   ```
   handlers:
   - url: /.*
     secure: always
     redirect_http_response_code: 301
     script: auto
   ```
7. If the line `secure: always` is present, the application is compliant.
8. If the line is missing, this indicates that connections are not being enforced over HTTPS, and steps must be taken to add this line to ensure secure connections.

**Expected secure configuration:** The `secure: always` directive should be present under the `handlers` section in the `app.yaml` file.

**How to confirm compliance:** Once you verify that the `secure: always` line is in the `app.yaml` file, you can confirm compliance. If any other directives besides this can also enforce HTTPS but lack the `secure: always`, the application is still deemed non-compliant until corrected.
